question_text: A student drops a ball from a height of 10 m. At the moment of release, the ball has gravitational potential energy of 50 J. After falling 5 m, the ball has gravitational potential energy of 25 J. Assuming no air resistance, calculate the kinetic energy of the ball after it has fallen 5 m.
explanation: Using conservation of energy: total energy at start = total energy during fall. Initial energy = 50 J (all gravitational potential energy). At 5 m height, gravitational potential energy = 25 J. Since energy is conserved and no energy is lost to air resistance, the 'missing' 25 J must have been converted to kinetic energy (50 J - 25 J = 25 J).
